title: The Logistics of the Mongol Conquests
author: From: The Secret History of the Mongols
content: Speed in communication was their ultimate weapon. The yams (postal) system allowed them to govern an empire larger than the sum of its parts.

title: The Fall of the Roman Republic
author: From: The Roman Historians
content: The Republic fell when its institutions could no longer handle the scale of its own expansion. Complexity grew faster than the ability to manage it.

title: The Enlightenment of Saladin
author: From: The Crusades
content: Even in total war, Saladin understood the value of diplomatic dignity and the long-term benefit of maintaining a reputation for mercy and honor.

title: The Meiji Restoration
author: From: Japanese History
content: The Japanese successfully adopted Western industrial technology while preserving the Japanese spirit. They did not discard their foundation; they updated their tools.
action: Modernize the tool, preserve the principle.

title: The Diplomacy of Talleyrand
author: From: French History
content: He survived every regime change by never being 'too' identified with one faction. Flexibility is the ultimate survival trait in shifting environments.
